nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool for content creation
nodes.label_negative: AI as final content producer without human refinement
nodes.grounding_span: I mainly use AI for basic content creation and ideation, from which I then refine and create finalised documents

nodes.label: Human judgment over AI suggestions
nodes.label_negative: AI suggestion infeasible due to cost, repetition, mismatch with people or culture
nodes.grounding_span: The types of factors that make certain suggestions appropriate include how costly they might be to implement, whether we have tried the solution before, personal judgement as to whether they would work with the specific people involved and whether the ideas fit within our organisations culture

nodes.label: AI for internal tasks not reaching external audience
nodes.label_negative: AI use for external audience without human final review
nodes.grounding_span: Any information that is going to an audience beyond myself I would always want to have the final draft and decision on content

nodes.label: AI as collaborator for iterative refinement
nodes.label_negative: Delegating complete tasks to AI independently
nodes.grounding_span: Definitely collaborate on a task - I will start with a request and then refine over a series of questions and providing more content for the AI model

nodes.label: AI for mental load reduction
nodes.label_negative: AI as crutch that replaces own thinking
nodes.grounding_span: It can also become difficult to generate ideas (mental fatigue from working, trying to multi-task etc.)

nodes.label: AI requiring skill and attention to use well
nodes.label_negative: Focus on maintaining skills AI can take over
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think so much as to maintaining my skills where AI can take over but more thinking about growing my skills in areas that AI may not be able to assist with

nodes.label: AI adoption in workplace
nodes.grounding_span: the need for people and organisations to build up trust for AI and what it can do before it can be widely adopted

nodes.label: Professional reputation and credibility
nodes.grounding_span: it would not reflect well on me or my company

nodes.label: Effective change management and stakeholder engagement
nodes.grounding_span: Leadership (influencing people and growing teams) and stakeholder engagement are areas that I see as important for me to focus on

nodes.label: Professional growth and skill maintenance
nodes.grounding_span: growing my skills in areas that AI may not be able to assist with

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: My experiences are mostly satisfied ones

nodes.label: Concern about AI's impact on audience behavior
nodes.grounding_span: Any information that is going to an audience beyond myself I would always want to have the final draft and decision on content

nodes.label: Openness to increasing AI use in future
nodes.grounding_span: I need to make an effort to learn and understand more about what AI can do in my current role

nodes.label: Systematic filtering of AI suggestions through contextual factors
nodes.grounding_span: The types of factors that make certain suggestions appropriate include how costly they might be to implement, whether we have tried the solution before, personal judgement as to whether they would work with the specific people involved and whether the ideas fit within our organisations culture

nodes.label: Collaborative iterative refinement — works back and forth with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I will start with a request and then refine over a series of questions and providing more content for the AI model
